extraction.one_line_summary: GILBERT Philippe won from 47-rider wind split that formed from the gun, with Quintana gaining 5:19 on Roglic to jump to 2nd GC.
tactical_patterns: Deceuninck Quickstep forced split immediately using 54-55 tooth chainrings pre-adapted for crosswinds, revealing pre-stage wind reconnaissance.
Movistar attacked FROM the front group to shell UAE/Jumbo/Astana domestiques, then sat up—isolating rivals rather than chasing their own leader.
Teams allowed 6+ minute gap to persist until 85km because chasing squads lost workers to Movistar's counter-attacks on uncategorised climbs.
Bennett launched 500m out in massive headwind finish but Gilbert could sit wheel and rest before countering, showing tailwind negated early jump.

rider_signals.QUINTANA Nairo: positive: Best GC rider in 47-man split gained 5:19 despite being 7+ minutes down pre-stage, proving crosswind attentiveness
rider_signals.ROGLIC Primoz: negative: Jumbo Visma caught out by immediate split despite GC lead, required Astana/UAE bailout for entire stage
rider_signals.POGACAR Tadej: neutral: UAE committed full team to chase for entire day protecting 3rd GC, revealing weakness in wind reading
rider_signals.KELDERMAN Wilco: positive: Sunweb placed him in split despite no obvious GC threat, moved to 7th showing team's wind anticipation
rider_signals.KNOX James: positive: Deceuninck protected GC domestique in split while sacrificing sprinter Jakobsen, pragmatic resource allocation to reach 8th overall
rider_signals.VALVERDE Alejandro: negative: Lost 30 seconds previous stage and missed crucial split today, age showing in wind awareness
rider_signals.SANCHEZ Luis Leon: negative: Visibly unhappy over radio when ordered to drop back from break to chase, poor team discipline under pressure
rider_signals.BENNETT Sam: negative: In winning break but mistimed 500m sprint into headwind, lacked power to hold off Gilbert's counter
